task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code correctly handles the edge case of an empty list.
trace: I approached this task by first checking if the input list is empty, in which case I return None. Then, I sort the list and calculate the index of the middle element (or elements) based on the length of the sorted list. If the length is odd, I return the middle element directly. If the length is even, I average the two middle elements to find the median. I also considered an edge case where a tied value can occur at the middle position when the length is even, but since the question doesn't explicitly state that this should be handled differently from averaging the two middle values, I chose to follow the original task criteria.
